violations.text: Bismark's were observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, Bismark's were put in unit less than 2 hours prior, Bismark's were relocated to another unit at time of inspection. At the time of inspection, the ambient temperature of the unit was 45F. PIC adjusted the unit, after adjustment ambient temperature was observed at 41F. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/15/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the bakery speed racks have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2026
The ceiling in the bakery area is in poor repair, inspector observed missing tile with exposed wood. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2026

At the time of inspection, the 2 Door Reach-In Cooler located in the prep area was not in good repair. The unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 48 degrees Fahrenheit with food temps at 43 to 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Per the PIC, temperature was observed prior to opening at 38 degrees Fahrenheit. Foods were moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. Use of the 2 Door Reach-In Cooler in the prep area is to be discontinued until repaired/replaced. (Per the PIC, a replacement refrigeration unit is on order.) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for the following time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods... - Recently made Pastry Cream was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep (Covered Buckets). Cream was moved to freezers and uncovered at the time of inspection. - 2 Pans of potatoes were observed cooling in the prep area 2 door cooler with one pan directly on top of the a covered pan of potatoes. Pans of potatoes were separated and moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed in need of repair/adjustment... - The 2 door cooler in the kitchen prep area was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 45 degrees Fahrenheit. At the time of inspection, potatoes were observed cooling in the refrigeration unit at 75 degrees Fahrenheit. Potatoes were removed and placed into a freezer to continue to properly cool. All other foods moved to colder refrigeration. - The bakery chest cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit was also observed to have torn gaskets. Unit is to discontinued for use of refrigeration for Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods until serviced/repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following equipment was observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - Internal surfaces of refrigeration equipment - Surfaces of the cooking equipment - Bakery speed racks - Surfaces of Dry Ingredients storage containers New Violation. Correct By: 05/25/2023

The following areas of the Physical Facilities were observed to be in poor repair at the time of inspection... - The ceiling in the bakery area was observed to have missing tile/covering exposing the structure above - The coving at the base of the wall was observed to be missing in several areas The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
